title: Numbers, Shapes, and Measurement Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A word or symbol that shows how many things there are.
choices: number
million
fact
rounds.answer: number
rounds.explanation: number: A word or symbol that shows how many things there are.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Putting numbers together to find how many you have altogether.
choices: number
million
addition
rounds.answer: addition
rounds.explanation: addition: Putting numbers together to find how many you have altogether.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: How big or how small something is when you look at it.
choices: million
size
number
rounds.answer: size
rounds.explanation: size: How big or how small something is when you look at it.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A very big number written 1,000,000, or one thousand thousands.
rounds.answer: million

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Putting numbers together to find how many you have altogether.
rounds.answer: addition

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A unit of length equal to one hundred centimeters.
rounds.answer: meter

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: My favourite number is seven.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: It is a fact that a week has seven days.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Draw a straight line across the page.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence explains a reason with 'because'?
choices: I used multiplication today.
I used multiplication because it is faster than adding.
Multiplication and addition are both useful maths operations.
Use multiplication now.
rounds.answer: I used multiplication because it is faster than adding.
rounds.explanation: Because joins the effect to its cause, so it tells us why.

rounds.prompt: Choose the missing word from A Pattern Poem: “Five, six, shapes and sticks, _____ and round, patterns found!”
choices: Subtraction
Multiplication
Division
Straight
rounds.answer: Straight
rounds.explanation: The poem sets straight lines against round ones, so Straight completes the rhyme.

rounds.prompt: Choose the missing word from Maths Is Everywhere: “When Amal helps her mother at the market, she uses _____ to add the prices.”
choices: addition
subtraction
million
height
rounds.answer: addition
rounds.explanation: Adding prices together is addition, which is why the text names that operation.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: Which maths word does Amal use to add the prices at the market?
choices: She uses addition to add the prices at the market.
One, two, three, four, five, six.
One hundred thousand comes just before one million.
rounds.answer: She uses addition to add the prices at the market.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: She uses addition to add the prices at the market.

rounds.prompt: What repeats in every line of the pattern poem to make the pattern?
choices: One, two, three, four, five, six.
She asked whether they could find the right pattern to solve the number puzzle.
Two counting numbers begin the line and a rhyming word ends it.
rounds.answer: Two counting numbers begin the line and a rhyming word ends it.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Two counting numbers begin the line and a rhyming word ends it.

rounds.prompt: Which number comes just before one million in Numbers Big and Small?
choices: He multiplied 25 shells by 10 bowls to reach 250 shells.
One hundred thousand comes just before one million.
Two counting numbers begin the line and a rhyming word ends it.
rounds.answer: One hundred thousand comes just before one million.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: One hundred thousand comes just before one million.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Measure and Tell
rounds.target: This is a / an ______. Its height is about ______.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 3 — Guess My Pattern
Speaking 5 — Explain It with Because
rounds.target: We multiplied because it is faster than adding.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Division helps us share dates fairly.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: What size are your shoes?

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: My height is 120 centimeters.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'subtraction' mean in this unit?
choices: Taking one number away from another to see what is left.
A word or symbol that shows how many things there are.
A very big number written 1,000,000, or one thousand thousands.
rounds.answer: Taking one number away from another to see what is left.
rounds.explanation: subtraction: Taking one number away from another to see what is left.

rounds.prompt: What does 'meter' mean in this unit?
choices: A word or symbol that shows how many things there are.
A very big number written 1,000,000, or one thousand thousands.
A unit of length equal to one hundred centimeters.
rounds.answer: A unit of length equal to one hundred centimeters.
rounds.explanation: meter: A unit of length equal to one hundred centimeters.

rounds.prompt: What does 'million' mean in this unit?
choices: Something that is true and can be checked or proven.
A very big number written 1,000,000, or one thousand thousands.
A word or symbol that shows how many things there are.
rounds.answer: A very big number written 1,000,000, or one thousand thousands.
rounds.explanation: million: A very big number written 1,000,000, or one thousand thousands.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'division'?
choices: Noun
Verb
Adjective
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: division is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'weight'?
choices: Verb
Adjective
Noun
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: weight is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'pattern'?
choices: Adjective
Noun
Verb
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: pattern is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: Something that is true and can be checked or proven.
A design or sequence of things that repeats in the same order.
Putting numbers together to find how many you have altogether.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: Adding the same number many times to find a total quickly.
Splitting a number into equal parts so everyone gets the same.
How big or how small something is when you look at it.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: How tall a person or thing is from bottom to top.
How heavy something is when you put it on the scales.
A word or symbol that shows how many things there are.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: In The Mystery of the Million Shells, how many shells did the teams count in total?
choices: 250 shells
500 shells
150 shells
375 shells
rounds.answer: 375 shells
rounds.explanation: Amal reports 375, and Teacher Yasmin confirms the total is 375 shells.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence gives a clear instruction, using an imperative?
choices: Measure the height of your desk.
She measures the rope every morning.
The desk is one meter high.
Will you measure it tomorrow?
rounds.answer: Measure the height of your desk.
rounds.explanation: An imperative starts with the verb and gives the order straight away.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence uses 'have got' correctly for one person?
choices: They has got a meter stick.
We has got ten bowls.
She has got a new ruler.
He have got a bicycle.
rounds.answer: She has got a new ruler.
rounds.explanation: He, she and it take has got, but we and they take have got.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: What does the word 'million' mean in this unit?
choices: A very big number written 1,000,000, or one thousand thousands.
A unit of length equal to one hundred centimeters.
Going the same way without bending or curving, like a ruler edge.
Splitting a number into equal parts so everyone gets the same.
rounds.answer: A very big number written 1,000,000, or one thousand thousands.
rounds.explanation: One million counts things, so it is a number, not a length.

rounds.prompt: What does the word 'measure' mean in this unit?
choices: A design or sequence of things that repeats in the same order.
How far it is from one place to another place.
To find out the size, length or amount of something.
Taking one number away from another to see what is left.
rounds.answer: To find out the size, length or amount of something.
rounds.explanation: Measure is the action; the others name a pattern, a distance and subtraction.

rounds.prompt: What does the word 'pattern' mean in this unit?
choices: How heavy something is when you put it on the scales.
A design or sequence of things that repeats in the same order.
Something that is true and can be checked or proven.
Adding the same number over and over again to find a total quickly.
rounds.answer: A design or sequence of things that repeats in the same order.
rounds.explanation: A pattern repeats in order, like red, blue, red, blue on the classroom tiles.
